name: Jerusalem
description: The holy city, capital of ancient Israel and Judah. Site of the Temple built by Solomon. Center of Jewish worship and later the Early Church.
significance: City of David, Temple Mount, Crucifixion and Resurrection of Jesus

name: Bethlehem
description: Birthplace of King David and Jesus Christ. A small town in the hill country of Judah.
significance: Birthplace of Jesus and King David

name: Nazareth
description: Hometown of Jesus, located in the hills of lower Galilee. Where the angel Gabriel announced Jesus' birth to Mary.

name: Galilee
description: Northern region of Israel where Jesus grew up and conducted most of his ministry. Area around the Sea of Galilee.

name: Sea of Galilee
description: Freshwater lake in northern Israel where Jesus walked on water, calmed storms, and called his first disciples.
significance: Site of many miracles in Jesus' ministry

name: Jericho
description: Ancient city near the Jordan River, known for its walls falling down under Joshua. One of the oldest continuously inhabited cities.
significance: Walls of Jericho, healing of blind Bartimaeus

name: Jordan River
description: Major river forming the eastern border of Canaan. Where the Israelites crossed into the Promised Land and where Jesus was baptized by John.
significance: Crossing into Promised Land, baptism of Jesus

name: Mount Sinai
description: Mountain in the Sinai Peninsula where Moses received the Ten Commandments from God.

name: Mount of Olives
description: Mountain ridge east of Jerusalem. Jesus ascended to heaven from here and will return here according to prophecy. Site of the Garden of Gethsemane.
significance: Ascension, Olivet Discourse, Garden of Gethsemane

name: Golgotha
description: The hill outside Jerusalem where Jesus was crucified. Also called Calvary.

name: Egypt
description: Ancient civilization along the Nile. The Israelites were enslaved here before the Exodus. Jesus and his family fled here after his birth.
significance: Israelite slavery and Exodus, flight of Holy Family

name: Babylon
description: Capital of the Babylonian Empire. The Jews were exiled here for 70 years after the destruction of Jerusalem.

name: Nineveh
description: Capital of the Assyrian Empire. The prophet Jonah was sent to warn this city to repent.
significance: Jonah's mission, repentance of the Ninevites

name: Damascus
description: Ancient city and capital of Syria. Paul (Saul) was converted on the road to Damascus. One of the oldest continuously inhabited cities.

name: Antioch
description: Major city in ancient Syria where believers were first called Christians. Base for Paul's missionary journeys.
significance: First called Christians, early church center

name: Rome
description: Capital of the Roman Empire. Paul was martyred here. The Book of Romans was written to the church in Rome.
significance: Capital of Roman Empire, Paul's martyrdom

name: Ephesus
description: Major city in Asia Minor. Paul lived here for three years. Home of the Temple of Artemis. Letter to the Ephesians.
significance: Paul's ministry, early church, Temple of Artemis

name: Corinth
description: Major Greek city. Paul wrote two letters to the church here. Key stop on his missionary journeys.

name: Athens
description: Intellectual center of ancient Greece. Paul preached at the Areopagus (Mars Hill) about the Unknown God.

name: Philippi
description: Roman colony in Macedonia. Paul founded a church here. Wrote the letter to the Philippians while imprisoned.
significance: First church in Europe, letter to the Philippians

name: Troas
description: Port city in northwest Asia Minor. Paul received the Macedonian call to preach in Europe.

name: Thessalonica
description: Major Macedonian city. Paul preached here and wrote two letters to the church.

name: Patmos
description: Aegean island where John received the Revelation and wrote the Book of Revelation while exiled.
significance: Writing of the Book of Revelation

name: Cana of Galilee
description: Village in Galilee where Jesus performed his first miracle — turning water into wine at a wedding.

name: Capernaum
description: Town on the Sea of Galilee where Jesus based his ministry. Called his own city. Site of many miracles.

name: Bethany
description: Village near Jerusalem where Lazarus, Martha, and Mary lived. Jesus raised Lazarus from the dead here.
significance: Raising of Lazarus, home of Mary and Martha

name: Samaria
description: Region between Galilee and Judea. Jesus spoke with the Samaritan woman at Jacob's Well. Early church spread here after persecution.
significance: Samaritan woman at the well, early church expansion

name: Ur of the Chaldeans
description: Ancient Sumerian city in Mesopotamia. Birthplace of Abraham, the father of the Israelites.

name: Haran
description: City in Mesopotamia where Abraham lived after leaving Ur and before journeying to Canaan.
significance: Abraham's stop on journey to Canaan

name: Sodom
description: City destroyed by God along with Gomorrah for its wickedness. Location near the Dead Sea.

name: Gomorrah
description: City destroyed alongside Sodom for its wickedness.

name: Hebron
description: City in the hill country of Judah where Abraham, Sarah, Isaac, Rebekah, Jacob, and Leah are buried in the Cave of Machpelah. David ruled from here before Jerusalem.
significance: Cave of Machpelah, David's first capital

name: Beersheba
description: Southernmost major city of ancient Israel. Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob all lived here. 'From Dan to Beersheba' marks Israel's extent.
significance: Southern boundary of Israel, patriarchs' home

name: Dan
description: Northernmost city of ancient Israel. Site of one of Jeroboam's golden calves. 'From Dan to Beersheba' marks Israel's extent.

name: Shiloh
description: Religious center of Israel before Jerusalem. The Tabernacle and Ark of the Covenant were located here during the time of Joshua and the Judges.
significance: Location of the Tabernacle, Samuel's calling

name: Bethel
description: Holy site near Jerusalem where Jacob dreamed of a ladder reaching to heaven. Later a center of worship for the northern kingdom.

name: Gilgal
description: First camp of the Israelites after crossing the Jordan River. Joshua set up twelve stones here. Saul was made king here.
significance: First camp in Promised Land, Saul made king

name: Ai
description: Canaanite city conquered by Joshua after the initial defeat due to Achan's sin. Near Bethel.

name: Gibeon
description: City where Joshua commanded the sun to stand still. The Gibeonites tricked Israel into a treaty.

name: Ashdod
description: One of the five Philistine capital cities. Where the Ark of the Covenant was placed in the temple of Dagon.
significance: Philistine city, Ark in temple of Dagon

name: Gath
description: Philistine city, home of Goliath whom David defeated. One of the five Philistine capitals.

name: Gaza
description: Major Philistine city. Samson carried away its gates and later died here pulling down the temple of Dagon.

name: Megiddo
description: Strategic fortress city. Site of many battles. The word Armageddon (Har Megiddo) comes from this location.
significance: Armageddon — final battle location in Revelation

name: Hazor
description: Major Canaanite city conquered by Joshua. Later fortified by Solomon. Largest tell in Israel.

name: Ezion-Geber
description: Port city at the northern tip of the Red Sea (Gulf of Aqaba). King Solomon built ships here for trade.

name: Mount Carmel
description: Mountain ridge overlooking the Mediterranean. Elijah challenged the prophets of Baal here in a dramatic showdown.
significance: Elijah's contest with prophets of Baal

name: Mount Gerizim
description: Mountain in Samaria where blessings were pronounced (Deuteronomy). Sacred site for Samaritans. Jesus spoke with the Samaritan woman near here.
significance: Mount of Blessings, Samaritan worship site

name: Mount Ebal
description: Mountain opposite Mount Gerizim where curses were pronounced (Deuteronomy). Joshua built an altar here.

name: Dead Sea
description: Salt lake at the lowest elevation on Earth. The Dead Sea region was home to Sodom and Gomorrah. David hid in caves near here.
significance: Lowest point on Earth, location of Sodom and Gomorrah

name: Nile River
description: Longest river in the world. Central to Egyptian civilization. Where Moses was placed in a basket as a baby. Plagues of Egypt involved the Nile.
significance: Moses in the bulrushes, plagues of Egypt

name: Red Sea
description: Sea miraculously parted by God so the Israelites could escape from Egypt.
significance: Parting of the Red Sea, Exodus from Egypt

name: Wilderness of Paran
description: Desert region in the Sinai Peninsula where the Israelites wandered. Kadesh-Barnea was located here.

name: Wilderness of Zin
description: Desert region in the Negev. Miriam died here. Moses struck the rock to bring water here.
significance: Israelite wanderings, water from the rock

name: Philistia
description: Coastal region of the five Philistine cities (Gaza, Ashkelon, Ashdod, Ekron, Gath). Constant enemy of Israel.
significance: Philistine territory, judges and kings conflicts

name: Garden of Eden
description: The original garden where God placed Adam and Eve. Location unknown, traditionally associated with the Tigris-Euphrates river system.

name: Mount Ararat
description: Mountain in modern Turkey where Noah's Ark came to rest after the Flood.

name: Babel (Babylon)
description: Site of the Tower of Babel where God confused human languages. Later became Babylon.
significance: Tower of Babel, confusion of languages

name: Susa
description: Capital of the Persian Empire. Setting for the Book of Esther. Where Nehemiah served as cupbearer to the king.

name: Persia
description: Ancient empire. Cyrus the Great conquered Babylon and allowed the Jews to return to Jerusalem.

name: Tarshish
description: Distant port city (likely in Spain). Jonah attempted to flee to Tarshish from God's call. Source of wealth in trade.

name: Lystra
description: City in Asia Minor where Paul healed a crippled man and was mistaken for Hermes. Timothy's hometown.

name: Derbe
description: City in Asia Minor visited by Paul on his missionary journeys.

name: Cyprus
description: Island in the eastern Mediterranean. Barnabas's home. Paul and Barnabas preached here on the first missionary journey.

name: Crete
description: Island in the Mediterranean. Paul left Titus here to establish churches.
